title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1, contingent on salary-cap adjustments.
A.J. Brown, a three-time Pro Bowler, is linked to the Patriots due to his connection with head coach Mike Vrabel.
The Eagles reportedly seek a first-round draft pick for Brown, and discussions could change based on demands and negotiations.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' a musical biopic streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' directed by Nia DaCosta, is available on Netflix.
Ken Burns' documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' featuring Don Henley is streaming on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox break a five-game losing streak with a 5-2 win over Padres in home opener.
Caleb Durbin ends an 0-for-34 slump with an RBI single, earning cheers from Fenway Park fans.
Willson Contreras hits first Red Sox home run, contributing to a pivotal three-run sixth inning.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots begin phase one of offseason workouts on April 20, focusing on conditioning drills and team meetings.
Organized Team Activities (OTAs) for the Patriots are scheduled for May 27-29, June 1-2, 4, 8-9, and 11.
Mandatory minicamp runs from June 15-17, concluding the voluntary offseason program before July's training camp.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's mock draft predicts Patriots selecting edge rushers Cashius Howell and Gabe Jacas in the first two rounds.
Howell, from Texas A&M, posted 11.5 sacks last season and is compared to NFL's Will McDonald IV.
Jacas, from Illinois, is noted for his run defense and resembles the style of player Matthew Judon.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox claimed a 5-2 victory over the San Diego Padres in their home opener at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, and Willson Contreras hit a key home run to lead the Red Sox.
The win marked a fresh start for Boston, improving their record to 2-5 after a difficult start to the season.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover plans to implement a body-worn camera program for police following an officer-involved shooting incident.
Officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted after being shot by a colleague during a confrontation at her home.
North Andover will seek grant funding to offset costs, joining 144 local police departments with state-funded camera programs.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park as a Padres player, receiving a standing ovation from Red Sox fans.
Bogaerts signed an 11-year, $280 million contract with San Diego in 2022, aiming for the Padres' first title.
Bogaerts highlighted pressure on 21-year-old Roman Anthony, reflecting on Boston's high expectations and winning culture.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail spans 2.5 miles and connects 16 historical sites, drawing over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England unveils Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, starting April 11 for season pass holders.
Portsmouth, N.H., hosts Restaurant Week offering special prix fixe menus from April 9-18 at over 30 restaurants.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton's noise complaint against Warwick neighbors has reached the Rhode Island Supreme Court.
Carlton alleges unlawful noise levels and harassment from neighbors' industrial property near her Warwick home.
Neighbors Artak Avagyan and Lee Beausoleil appealed an injunction limiting noise levels and activities.
